local_names.hindi: बुन बो ह्यू (वियतनामी मसालेदार बीफ नूडल सूप)

aliases: Hue-style beef and pork noodle soup
बुन बो ह्यू (वियतनामी मसालेदार बीफ नूडल सूप)

english: Hue-style beef and pork noodle soup
hindi: बुन बो ह्यू (वियतनामी मसालेदार बीफ नूडल सूप)

region_of_origin: Vietnam — Hue (Thừa Thiên Huế province, Central Vietnam)
food_subgroup: Spicy Vietnamese beef-pork noodle soup with thick round rice noodles (bún), lemongrass-fermented shrimp broth, sliced beef, pork knuckle, and Vietnamese sausage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Bun Bo Hue specifically. Closest parent would be a generic noodle soup concept. No code assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Vietnamese beef noodle soup (closest parent — Bun Bo Hue is a distinct Central Vietnamese preparation not individually coded in FoodEx2)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CMF used as closest parent for Vietnamese beef noodle soup. Bun Bo Hue is not individually enumerated in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Bun Bo Hue. Not clinically coded as a distinct food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of Bun Bo Hue weighs approximately 550g, comprising roughly 300g of lemongrass-fermented shrimp broth, 130g of thick round rice noodles, and 120g of mixed toppings including sliced beef shank, pork knuckle, and Vietnamese sausage.

nutrient_source.title: FAO/INFOODS Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia (SEA) — Bun Bo Hue / Vietnamese beef noodle soup
nutrient_source.organization: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations / INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: FAO/INFOODS SEA Table 2020 used as primary source for per-100g nutrient values for Vietnamese beef noodle soup. Values are consistent with the composition of Bun Bo Hue (thick round rice noodles, beef, pork, lemongrass-shrimp broth). Sodium value (300mg/100g) reflects the higher sodium load from fermented shrimp paste (mắm ruốc) characteristic of Bun Bo Hue versus plain pho. Secondary cross-reference: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual component validation (beef shank, pork, rice noodles).

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rice Noodles and Mixed Noodle Dishes — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate for thick round rice noodles (bún) based on laksa rice noodles and similar thick rice noodle preparations (GI 58–63)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bun Bo Hue as a complete dish. GI ~60 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for thick round rice noodles (bún-type, similar to laksa rice noodles, GI 58–63). Glycemic load per 550g bowl calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (60 × 55g) / 100 = 33.0. Available carbohydrate per 550g bowl = 10.0g/100g × 550g = 55g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content — approximately 1,650mg per bowl (300mg/100g × 550g), representing 82.5% of the 2,000mg daily sodium limit, driven primarily by fermented shrimp paste (mắm ruốc) and fish sauce in the broth
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should consume Bun Bo Hue only occasionally; leaving at least half the broth unconsumed reduces sodium intake by approximately 600–700mg per bowl, and requesting reduced shrimp paste from the vendor further lowers sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (GL ~33 per bowl) from 130g of thick round rice noodles (GI ~60) in a standard 550g serving, which constitutes a full carbohydrate meal equivalent
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should request a reduced noodle portion ('less noodles'), increase protein toppings (extra beef shank) and fibre-rich garnishes (beansprouts, banana blossom), and treat a full bowl as their primary carbohydrate allocation for that meal to manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (0.9g/100g, ~5g per bowl) from pork knuckle and Vietnamese sausage, combined with high sodium (1,650mg per bowl) which is the dominant cardiovascular concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit Bun Bo Hue to occasional consumption, leave the majority of the broth to reduce sodium and fat intake, and choose leaner beef shank over pork knuckle and sausage toppings where possible to reduce saturated fat and cholesterol load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium from fermented shrimp paste is a concern for patients on corticosteroids; pork knuckle collagen peptides (glycine, proline) provide Grade D evidence for connective tissue support
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients on corticosteroids should be cautious of the high sodium content (~1,650mg per bowl) and limit broth consumption; the pork knuckle collagen may offer minor joint-support benefit but evidence remains weak.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bun Bo Hue (Bún Bò Huế) is the bold, spicy noodle soup of Hue, the imperial capital of Central Vietnam. It is widely regarded as one of Vietnam's most complex and assertive noodle dishes, competing with Pho for national affection but distinguished by a fiercer, more aromatic flavour profile. The broth is built from pork and beef bones simmered for hours with a generous quantity of lemongrass — far more than used in pho — and seasoned with fermented shrimp paste (mắm ruốc), which imparts a pungent, deeply savoury umami character unique to this dish. Annatto oil gives the broth its characteristic brick-red colour. The noodles are thick, round rice noodles (bún), which are chewier and denser than the flat rice noodles of pho. Toppings include sliced beef shank, braised pork knuckle (chân giò — gelatinous and rich), and Vietnamese pork sausage (chả Huế). Garnishes typically include beansprouts, banana blossom, fresh herbs, lime, and chilli. Per 100g: 87 kcal, 4.7g protein, 2.5g fat, 10.0g carbohydrate, 300mg sodium. Per standard bowl (~550g): approximately 479 kcal, 26g protein, 14g fat, 55g carbohydrate, GI ~60, GL ~33.0, and ~1,650mg sodium. The sodium content is significantly higher than Pho Bo (~1,100mg per bowl) due to the fermented shrimp paste (mắm ruốc), which contributes approximately 400mg additional sodium compared to a plain fish-sauce broth. Leaving half the broth unconsumed saves approximately 600–700mg sodium. The dish is gluten-free and dairy-free. Primary nutrient source: FAO/INFOODS SEA Table 2020.
common_user_queries: Bun Bo Hue vs Pho sodium
bun bo hue pork knuckle noodle soup
Hue beef noodle soup nutrition facts

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced from FAO/INFOODS SEA Table 2020, an approved primary regional source for Southeast Asian dishes. Values are internally consistent and align with the known composition of Vietnamese beef-pork noodle soup. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for thick round rice noodles (GI 58–63), which is the closest approved GI source available. No direct GI measurement for Bun Bo Hue as a complete dish exists in the approved source set.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Bun Bo Hue not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA Table 2020 or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite dish as served (including broth) not reported in FAO/INFOODS SEA Table 2020. trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 — confirmed negligible/trace per FAO/INFOODS SEA source for this dish type. GI ~60 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for thick round rice noodles (bún-type, GI 58–63); no direct GI measurement for Bun Bo Hue as a complete dish exists in the approved source set. Glycemic load per 550g bowl = 33.0 (calculated: GI 60 × 55g available carbohydrate / 100). Sodium per bowl (~1,650mg) is substantially higher than Pho Bo (~1,100mg) due to fermented shrimp paste (mắm ruốc). The dish is not halal-certified as standard preparations contain pork knuckle and pork sausage. FoodOn URI not assigned — no exact FoodOn concept for Bun Bo Hue exists; closest_parent noted. FoodEx2 code A0CMF used as closest parent (Vietnamese beef noodle soup). Two portion entries exist in source data (550g and 600g); 550g used as default serving size as it is the more detailed and better-documented estimate.
